skill.name: programmatic-seo
skill.description: Design and evaluate programmatic SEO strategies for scalable page systems using templates, structured data, keyword patterns, quality gates, and indexation controls.
skill.summary: Plan scalable SEO page systems with feasibility scoring, template strategy, and indexation safeguards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged ten potential issues, but review found no executable code, network access, filesystem access, or secret handling in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by SEO prose and headings.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in SEO Text
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at several lines, but these lines contain skill metadata, headings, and programmatic SEO guidance. No cryptographic code, hashing calls, encryption routines, or credential handling were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible prose about SEO strategy and page system design. There is no code path or semantic evidence of cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Alert from Crawl Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to SEO crawl management advice. The line discusses avoiding crawl traps and does not instruct the agent to inspect the host system, run commands, enumerate files, or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is about search engine crawl management. No reconnaissance commands or operational host-inspection behavior are present.

content.value_statement: Programmatic SEO can create many weak pages when feasibility, data quality, and intent are not checked. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code evaluate fit and design scalable page systems with quality controls.
actual_capabilities: Scores programmatic SEO feasibility across search patterns, unique value, data quality, intent, competition, and operations.
Identifies when a page system should not proceed because it may create thin or risky content.
Designs keyword patterns, variable sets, URL structures, data requirements, and template sections.
Defines indexation rules, crawl controls, quality gates, and rollback criteria for scaled page launches.
Maps suitable playbooks such as comparisons, locations, directories, profiles, integrations, and glossaries.
Produces a structured strategy with opportunity summary, page system design, risks, and mitigations.
limitations: Does not run keyword tools, fetch SERP data, or validate search volume unless connected tools are separately available.
Does not implement pages, schemas, analytics, or CMS templates unless the user explicitly requests execution work.
Cannot guarantee rankings, traffic, indexing, or protection from search engine updates.
Depends on accurate business context, data sources, and competitive information supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Evaluate a New SEO Page Program
use_cases.description: Score whether a proposed page pattern has enough demand, unique value, and data quality to justify scaled publication.

use_cases.title: Design Scalable Page Templates
use_cases.description: Plan the URL pattern, data model, page sections, conditional content, and internal linking rules before engineering starts.

use_cases.title: Control Indexation Risk
use_cases.description: Create quality gates, noindex criteria, sitemap segmentation, and rollback rules for large SEO page inventories.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether programmatic SEO is a good fit for my idea: [describe product, audience, page type, and keyword pattern]. Give a feasibility score and explain the main risks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Page Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these programmatic SEO patterns for my business: [list patterns]. Score each one for demand, unique value, data quality, intent match, competition, and operational effort.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Page System
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a validated opportunity into a plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a programmatic SEO page system for [validated pattern]. Include URL structure, required data fields, template sections, conditional content, internal links, and indexation rules.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Launch Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this planned programmatic SEO launch: [summarize page count, data sources, templates, indexation criteria, and analytics]. Identify thin-content risks, crawl risks, quality gates, and rollback triggers.

output_examples.input: A marketplace wants city pages for every service category and location.
output: Recommendation to index only combinations with demand and complete local data.
Template sections for local inventory, pricing, reviews, service coverage, and alternatives.
Risks covering doorway pages, sparse locations, and stale availability data.

output_examples.input: A SaaS company wants comparison pages for hundreds of competitor and integration terms.
output: Prioritized playbook choice between comparisons and integrations.
Required data fields for features, pricing, supported workflows, and proof points.
Indexation rules for pages with enough differentiated evidence.
Mitigations for duplicate claims, outdated facts, and weak conversion intent.

output_examples.input: A content team wants to expand a glossary into thousands of pages.
output: Warning when term variations do not justify separate URLs.
Suggested consolidation rules for near-duplicate concepts.
Quality gates for definitions, examples, internal links, and expert review.
Alternative editorial clusters when scaled pages are not justified.

best_practices: Start with the feasibility index before designing templates or page counts.
Use unique, maintainable data that creates meaningful differences between pages.
Define indexation and rollback rules before publishing pages at scale.
anti_patterns: Generating many pages by swapping city, product, or keyword variables without unique value.
Indexing every possible URL combination before proving demand and content quality.
Treating the feasibility score as a traffic forecast or ranking guarantee.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create a programmatic SEO strategy, including feasibility scoring, page system design, indexation rules, risks, and mitigations.

faq.question: Can it build the pages for me?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on strategy and system design. It only moves into implementation when the user explicitly requests execution work.

faq.question: Does it perform live keyword research?
faq.answer: No. It can structure the research process, but it does not fetch live keyword or SERP data without separate connected tools.

faq.question: How does it reduce thin-content risk?
faq.answer: It checks unique value, data quality, intent fit, indexation criteria, and rollback triggers before recommending scaled publication.

faq.question: Which page types does it support?
faq.answer: It supports patterns such as directories, locations, comparisons, integrations, profiles, examples, glossaries, and curated collections.

faq.question: Is it suitable for non-technical teams?
faq.answer: Yes. The outputs are strategic and operational, so marketers, product managers, and SEO leads can use them before implementation.
